title: Label 309 verifier report
description: The structured report a Label 309 verifier (public or recipient role) emits for one verification run, whether the run started from a Cardano transaction reference or from caller-supplied record bytes plus a block-info tuple. This schema pins the report's MINIMUM contract — the fields the standard requires every report to carry; an implementation MAY add fields, so the report object and its sub-objects are deliberately OPEN maps (no additionalProperties: false). A `valid` verdict MUST NOT be reported when any error-severity code is present, and no severity may be softened to make a record pass.

verdict.title: Machine verdict
verdict.description: `failed` is reserved for record-attributable outcomes (integrity, structural, signature, Merkle-mismatch, and service-independence-violation classes, including METADATA_NOT_FOUND); condemnation requires evidence attributable to the record, so provider misbehaviour (TX_INTEGRITY_MISMATCH, URI_PROVIDER_INTEGRITY_MISMATCH) never produces it. `unverifiable` means no record-attributable error is present but a required check could not run or could not be attributed — network, policy, or provider-integrity reasons — including the commitment floor: content checking ran but no content commitment of the record was actually verified. `pending` is the below-confirmation-threshold outcome (INSUFFICIENT_CONFIRMATIONS); no result from a pending record may be presented as final.

exitCode.title: Process exit code
exitCode.description: The four-state mapping of the verdict, so callers can distinguish record-attributable failures from transient operational ones without parsing the report: valid → 0, failed → 1, unverifiable → 2, pending → 3. Exit codes 4 and higher denote verifier-host runtime failures that are not record-attributable and do not correspond to a verdict.

network.title: Network identifier
network.description: Names the network of the resolved transaction (for example `cardano:mainnet`), as established by the explorer chain the verifier is configured against — never taken from the record body, which carries no network value.

confirmationDepth.title: Resolved confirmation depth
confirmationDepth.description: Explorer-asserted depth in blocks: (resolving explorer's tip height) − (height of the block including the transaction) + 1, so a transaction in the tip block has depth exactly 1.

confirmationThreshold.title: Confirmation-depth threshold
confirmationThreshold.description: The deployment-policy threshold the resolved depth was compared against (RECOMMENDED >= 15 blocks, raised for high-value notarisation).

block_time.title: Block time
block_time.description: The POSIX timestamp, in integer seconds UTC, of the slot of the block that includes the transaction — the time T in the claim "this content existed on or before T". Explorer-asserted: deployments for which it is load-bearing MUST cross-check it across independent explorers.

block_slot.title: Block slot
block_slot.description: The slot number of the including block. Explorer-asserted; carried when available.

issues.title: Issue list
issues.description: The structural-validation issue list plus every verifier-layer code raised by the run, each with per-issue severity. Issues are sorted segment-wise by path (integer segments numerically, text segments by bytewise UTF-8 order, integer before text where kinds differ, prefix before extension); identical paths tie-break by error-code-registry order.

items.title: Per-item entries
items.description: One entry per record `items[]` element, positionally aligned: the per-claim content-check status and, for an `enc`-bearing item when the run's decryption keyring is non-empty, the decryption outcome. Empty exactly when the record carries no `items` array — every content claim the record makes must have an entry, so an unchecked claim cannot be silently omitted.

merkle.title: Per-commitment entries
merkle.description: One entry per record `merkle[]` element, positionally aligned: the per-claim content-check status of the list commitment (leaves-list acquisition, document validation, and root recompute). Empty exactly when the record carries no `merkle` array — every list commitment the record makes must have an entry.

auditTrail.title: Audit trail
auditTrail.description: A complete record of every outbound network call of the run — success, failure, and retry — captured by the single recording egress wrapper. A verifier that omits or pre-filters this trail cannot prove service-independence. An offline run (no outbound calls) carries an empty array.

issues.$comment: The severity contract: a valid verdict MUST NOT coexist with any error-severity issue, and an issue with omitted severity defaults to error — so under a valid verdict every issue must carry an explicit non-error severity.

allOf.$comment: A valid or pending verdict can only arise after the transaction resolved and the confirmation-depth comparison ran, so the chain facts the report must carry are necessarily known.

issue.title: Validation / verification issue
issue.description: One typed issue. The shape is shared by the structural validator and the verifier layer; verifier-layer codes that concern the run rather than a record location carry an empty path.

path.description: Ordered segments from the record root — text map keys and non-negative integer array indices, exactly as traversed (e.g. ["items", 0, "hashes", "sha2-256"]). The segment list is the wire/API form; a dotted string is a display rendering only, since map keys may themselves contain `.`.

code.description: A SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE code from the error-code registry; no lowercase synonyms or free-form reason strings.

severity.description: Defaults to `error` when omitted. A `valid` verdict MUST NOT be reported while any error-severity issue is present; warning- and info-severity issues are surfaced but never fail the record by themselves.

message.description: Human-readable explanation including the offending value.

content-check.title: Per-claim content-check status
content-check.description: The three-state status of one content claim, so an unchecked claim can never masquerade as a verified one: `checked` — bytes were obtained and every committed digest matched; `mismatched` — attributable fetched (or decrypted) bytes failed a commitment, a record-attributable integrity outcome; `not_checked` — the claim was not checked (`fetchContent` off, availability failure, unattributable fetched bytes, or the per-URI fetch ceiling).

decryption-outcome.title: Per-item decryption outcome
decryption-outcome.description: The recipient-verifier outcome for one `enc`-bearing item after every applicable keyring credential was attempted independently (one credential may open several items; different credentials may succeed on different items).

decrypted.description: Whether an applicable credential recovered the content-encryption key and the ciphertext opened end-to-end.

plaintextHashOk.description: The post-decryption recheck: every digest in the item's `hashes` map recomputed over the recovered plaintext. A concrete boolean whenever decryption ran to completion; `false` raises URI_INTEGRITY_MISMATCH and forces the record's verdict to `failed`.

code.description: The typed code describing why decryption did not succeed (for example WRONG_DECRYPTION_INPUT_SHAPE, WRONG_RECIPIENT_KEY, TAMPERED_HEADER, TAMPERED_CIPHERTEXT, KDF_DERIVATION_FAILED, CIPHERTEXT_UNAVAILABLE); the same code also appears in the issue list.

audit-entry.title: Audit-trail entry
audit-entry.description: One recorded outbound network call. Every call passes through the verifier's single recording egress wrapper, which enforces the closed purpose set (any other target is refused with URI_TARGET_FORBIDDEN) and hard-fails any call to a denyHosts entry with SERVICE_INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION.

status.description: The HTTP status code when a response was received; an implementation-defined transport-failure indicator (or null) when none was.

bytes.description: Byte count transferred for the call.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of the call in milliseconds.
